(2)If he walks closer to the obstacle, he may see the image of his shoes in the mirror.

(3)If he walks away from the obstacle, he may see the image of his shoes in the mirror.

As shown in the figure below, a letter ‘P’ is placed in front of a plane mirror.

Which of the following correctly shows the image of the letter ‘P’ as seen in the mirror?
hint: An image formed by a plane mirror is laterally inverted.

stem:
A clock is facing a plane mirror on a wall. The figure below shows the image of the clock in the mirror.

What is the actual time at this instant?
hint: An image formed by a plane mirror is laterally inverted. The clock looks like this:

stem:
Which of the following statements about the image formed by a plane mirror is/are correct?

(1)The image cannot be projected on a screen.

(2)Light rays entering the eyes do not come from the image.

(3)The image is behind the mirror.
hint: An image formed by a plane mirror is virtual. It cannot be projected on a screen. ∴ (1) is correct. Light rays entering the eyes are emitted or reflected from the object. They do not come from the image. ∴ (2) is correct. An image formed by a plane mirror is behind the mirror. (3) is correct.

stem: John is in front of a plane mirror. If the mirror moves away from him at a speed of 2 m s1, at what speed does his image move away from him?
hint: A1.50 m above the ground B1.63 m above the ground C1.75 m above the ground D1.83 m above the ground

stem:
A ray of light passes through media X, Y and Z as shown.

Which of the following statements about the three media are correct?

(1)Z is optically denser than X.

(2)Light travels the fastest in X.

(3)Y has the greatest refractive index.
hint: Let i1, i2 and r1, r2 be the angles of incidence and angles of refractions in the 1st and the 2nd refraction. r1 = i2 Apply Snell’s law. 1st refraction: nX sin i1 = nY sin r1 2nd refraction: nZ sin r2 = nY sin i2 = nY sin r1 = nX sin i1 i1 r2 sin i1 sin r2 nX nZ ∴ (1) is correct. Consider nX sin i1 = nY sin r1. i1 r1 sin i1 sin r1 nX nY The refractive index of X is the smallest. Therefore, light travels the fastest in X. ∴ (2) is correct. Consider nZ sin r2 = nY sin i2. i2 r2 sin i2 sin r2 nY nZ The refractive index of Y is the greatest. ∴ (3) is correct. 2. A ray of light travels from medi…

stem: The refractive index of X is 1.77. What is the refractive index of Y?
hint: By Snell’s law, nX sin X = nY sin Y 1.77 sin 36 = nY sin 42.8 nY = 1.53

stem:
Which of the following are examples of refraction?

(1)A chopstick appears bent when dipped in water.

(2)A distorted image of a tree is observed on a ruffled water surface.

(3)A goldfish in a tank appears nearer and bigger than it really is.
hint: The image of a tree observed on the water surface is formed by reflection of light. 4.

stem:
Which of the following statements about the laws of refraction is/are correct?

(1)The angle of refraction r is always equal to the angle of incidence i.

(2)The ratio of the sine of the angle of incidence i to the sine of the angle of refraction r is always equal to 1, i.e. .

(3)The incident ray, the refracted ray and the normal all lie in the same plane.
hint: 1the incident ray, the refracted ray and the normal all lie in the same plane. 2 = constant ∴ (1) and (2) are incorrect, (3) is correct. 8.

stem:
Three light rays enter an air bubble in water. C is the centre of the bubble. Which of the paths shown below is/are correct?

waterairwaterair

water

air

water

air
hint: When a light ray travels from water to air, its speed increases and hence it bends away from the normal. ∴ (3) is incorrect. When a light ray travels from air to water, its speed decreases and hence it bends towards the normal. ∴ (1) is correct. When the angle of incidence of a light ray is 0°, it does not bend. ∴ (2) is correct.

stem:
When a beam of white light passes through a glass prism, a colour spectrum is formed. Which of the following statements about this phenomenon is/are correct?

(1)White light is composed of a mixture of colours.

(2)Different colours of light travel at different speeds in glass.

(3)Green light is refracted more than red light in passing through the prism.
hint: ∴ (1) and (2) are correct. Green light is refracted more than red light in passing through the prism. ∴ (3) is correct.

stem:

What is ?

She finds that the air bubble appears silvery. water air Angela light rays water air Angela light rays

stem:
Which of the following instruments make(s) use of total internal reflection of light?

(1)Optical fibre

(2)Plane mirror

(3)Prisms in a periscope

A ray of light travels from medium X to medium Y and total internal reflection occurs as shown.

medium Xmedium Y50medium Xmedium Y50

medium X

medium Y

50

medium X

medium Y

50

Which of the following statements must be correct?

(1)The critical angle for the interface between medium X and medium Y is 40.

(2)Light travels faster in medium Y than in medium X.

(3)Medium Y is air.
hint: Angle of incidence = 90° - 50° = 40°

stem:
Since total internal reflection occurs, the critical angle for the interface between medium X and medium Y is smaller than 40°.

∴ (1) is incorrect.

Since total internal reflection occurs, medium X is optically denser than Y. Therefore, light travels faster in medium Y than in medium X.

∴ (2) is correct.

We cannot conclude that medium Y is air.

∴ (3) is incorrect.

What is/are the advantage(s) of right-angled prisms over plane glass mirrors in making periscopes?

(1)The image is erect.

(2)Multiple images are not formed.

(3)The image is of the same size as the object.
hint: ∴ (2) is correct. Images formed by a plane glass mirror and a prism are both erect and of the same size as the object. ∴ (1) and (3) are incorrect. 7.

stem:
When a light ray travels from glass to an optically less dense medium (air), and the angle of incidence is larger than the critical angle, total internal reflection occurs.

∴ (3) is correct.

As is smaller than the critical angle, total internal reflection does not occur at Q. The ray bends away from the normal when it travels from plastic to an optically less dense medium (air).

Which of the following statements about total internal reflection of light is/are correct?

(1)It occurs only when light travels from glass to air.

(2)It will never occur if light travels from air to glass.

(3)It will occur if light travels from a medium with a refractive index of 1.6 to air at an angle of incidence of 35.
hint: ∴ (1) is incorrect while (2) is correct. For (3), nm sin m = na sin a 1.6 sin C = 1 sin 90 C = 38.7 As the angle of incidence is smaller than the critical angle, total internal reflection will not occur. ∴ (3) is incorrect. 50.

stem:
As shown below, a small fish hides under the centre of a floating circular dish with a diameter of 2 m. If the fish moves vertically downwards from the water surface, how far can it move down the water before somebody above the water can see it?

Given: refractive index of water = 1.33
hint: Light from the fish is totally internally reflected when the angle of incidence is larger than the critical angle. nw sin w= na sin a 1.33 sin C = 1 sin 90 C= 48.8 Let d be the maximum distance that the fish can move down before somebody above the water can see it. tan 48.8 = d = 0.88 m

stem:
Which of the following statements about lens L shown in the figure below is/are correct?

principal axis

d

principal axis

d

(1)Lens L is a convex lens.

(2)Lens L has only one principal focus.

(3)The focal length of lens L equals distance d.
hint: Convex lens converges parallel rays. ∴ (1) is correct. Each lens has two principal foci, one on each side of it. ∴ (2) is incorrect. Focal length is the distance of the focus from the optical centre but not from the edge of the lens. ∴ (3) is incorrect.

stem:
Rays emerged after rays y and z pass through the lens should be above that for ray x. Rays emerged after ray w passes through the lens should be below that for ray x.

∴ C and D are incorrect. A is correct.

Which of the following statements about the image formed by a convex lens is/are correct?

(1)If the image is virtual, it must be erect.

(2)If the image is real, it can be either erect or inverted.

(3)If the image is real, it can be either magnified or diminished.
hint: A real image formed by a convex lens is always inverted. ∴ (2) is incorrect.

stem:
An object is put in front of a lens and a magnified image is captured on a screen. Which of the following statements is/are correct?

(1)The lens is a convex lens.

(2)If the lens is moved further away from the object, the image will become smaller.

(3)If the lens is replaced by the same type of lens with a shorter focal length, the image will become smaller.
hint: As the object distance increases, the image becomes smaller as shown. ∴ (1) and (2) are correct.

stem:
Replacing the lens with one of a smaller focal length is effectively the same as increasing the object distance u as u increases relatively to f.

∴ (3) is correct.

If F and F' are the principal foci of a convex lens, which of the following ray diagrams is/are incorrect?

(1)

(2)

(3)
hint: ∴ (1) is incorrect. Parallel incident rays should converge on the focal plane of the lens after passing through the lens. ∴ (2) and (3) are incorrect.

stem:
When the object distance becomes larger than f, the image becomes magnified and inverted.

When the object distance becomes larger than 2f, the image becomes diminished and inverted.

The figure below shows an experiment set-up. A clear image is formed on the screen when the convex lens and the plane mirror are placed at the right position.

Which of the following statements about the experiment is/are correct?

(1)The focal length of the convex lens is equal to the distance between the convex lens and the lamp-housing.

(2)The brightness and shape of the image will not change if the plane mirror is moved a little bit backwards.

(3)The image will be larger if the experiment is repeated by using another convex lens with a shorter focal length.
hint: Parallel incident rays converge only after passing through a convex lens. ∴ (1) is correct. When rays from a distant object pass through a convex lens, a real image is formed on the focal plane. ∴ (2) and (3) are correct.

An object is placed 5 cm in front of a convex lens with a focal length of 3 cm. In applying the lens formula to find the position of the image, which of the following statements is/are correct?

(1)f is 3 cm.

(2)u is +5 cm.

(3)The image is 1.875 cm from the lens on the same side as the object.
hint: f is positive for a convex lens. Therefore, f is +3 cm. ∴ (1) is incorrect. u is always positive. ∴ (2) is correct. By , ∴ (3) is incorrect.

stem:
An object 7 cm tall is placed in front of a lens and the image formed is 5 cm tall. Which of the following may be the lens used?

(1)Convex lens of focal length 2.92 cm

(2)Convex lens of focal length 17.5 cm

(3)Concave lens of focal length 2.92 cm

(4)Concave lens of focal length 17.5 cm
hint: Let f be the foal length. If the lens is convex and the object distance is greater than 2f, a diminished image is formed. Since the object distance is not given, (1) and (2) may be used.

stem:
An image formed by a concave lens is always diminished.

∴ (3) and (4) may be used.

An object is placed in front of a convex lens as shown. A real image is formed and its magnification is 1.5. What is the focal length of the lens?
hint: A magnified real image formed by a convex lens must be inverted. Construct a ray diagram as shown by drawing an inverted image I with an image distance of 15 cm. From the ray diagram, focal length of the lens = 6 cm

stem:
The magnification of an image is 2, and the distance between the image and the lens is 8 cm. Which of the following may be the distance between the image and the object?

(1)4 cm

(2)8 cm

(3)12 cm
hint: By m = , u = = = 4 cm Since the image is magnified, the lens must be convex. There are two scenarios the image can be formed. Scenario 1: If the object distance is between f and 2f, the image and the object are on opposite sides of the lens. Distance between the image and the object = 4 + 8 = 12 cm Scenario 2: If the object distance is smaller than f, the image and the object are on the same side of the lens. Distance between the image and the object = 8 4 = 4 cm ∴ (1) and (3) are correct. (2) is incorrect.

stem:
An object is placed 10 cm in front of a lens and an erect image is formed 6 cm from the lens. Which of the following is the correct description of the lens?

Focal length

Type of the lens
hint: Since the image is erect and diminished, the lens is diverging (concave) and the image is virtual. By , The focal length of the lens is 15 cm.

stem:
A student holds a lens 3 cm above a piece of paper. The image of the letters ‘AT’ is shown in the figure below. Its magnification is 3.

lens

lens

Find the image distance and the focal length of the lens.

Image distance
hint: Since the image is erect and magnified, the lens is convex. By m = , image distance = mu = 3 3 = 9 cm Apply . An erect image formed by a convex lens must be virtual. Therefore, v = 9 cm. The focal length of the lens is 4.5 cm.

stem: A luminous point object is placed 3 cm above the principal axis of a converging lens of focal length 10 cm. Its image is 5 cm below the axis. How far is the object from the lens?
hint: For this object, By , The object is 16 cm from the lens.

stem:
Which of the following statements about lenses is/are correct?

(1)Convex and concave lenses form images by refraction.

(2)Convex lenses are thinner in the middle.

(3)Concave lenses are diverging lenses.
hint: Light rays are bent due to refraction when entering and leaving a lens. ∴ (1) is correct. Convex lenses are thicker in the middle while concave lenses are thinner in the middle. ∴ (2) is incorrect. A concave lens diverges parallel light rays from a point. Therefore it is called a diverging lens. ∴ (3) is correct.

objectimage5 cmobjectimage5 cm

hint: By constructing the ray diagram as shown, the position of the lens is located and the focal length is found to be 15 cm.

(1)

(2)

(3)
hint: ∴ (1), (2) and (3) are incorrect.

stem: In the following diagram, intervals AB, BC, CD, DE and EF are of equal length. An object is placed at O. When a lens is put in front of the object, an image of the object is formed at I.
hint: ∴ (1) is incorrect.

stem:
For option (2), a concave lens should form an image within a focal length from the lens. The image should be on the same side as the object.

∴ (2) is correct.

For option (3), the object distance is between f and 2f, so the image distance should be greater than 2f. The image and the object should be on opposite sides of the lens.

∴ (3) is incorrect.

Which of the following statements about the image formed by a concave lens is/are correct?

(1)It is always between the lens and object.

(2)It is always diminished.

(3)It will be formed at infinity if the object is located at the focus of the lens.
hint: An image formed by a concave lens is always diminished and it always lies between the lens and the object. ∴ (1) and (2) are correct. (3) is incorrect.

stem:
In the following diagram, lenses X and Y are made of the same material but have different shapes, while lenses X and Z are made of different materials but have the same shape.

n = 1.5

n = 1.5

n = 1.7

Which of the following statements is/are correct?

(1)X, Y and Z are diverging lenses.

(2)Y has a shorter focal length than X.

(3)Z has a shorter focal length than X.
hint: A diverging (concave lens) is thinner in the middle. ∴ (1) is correct. A lens with a larger curvature has a shorter focal length. Y has a larger curvature than X. ∴ (2) is correct. A lens with a greater refractive index has a shorter focal length. Z has a greater refractive index than X. ∴ (3) is correct.
